Not sure if she should have been the one saying thanks. She made my day, and the day hadn’t even started yet. It was still early in the morning, maybe around 7 am, when Rayla and I both left for our duties.

As much as I would have loved to go to Zara, I had more pressing matters at the moment.

Getting stronger.

And for that…

“Dungeon exploration?” Elara raised an eyebrow as she raised her head to look at me from the papers she was reading on her desk.

“Yes, my lady. I believe that for the upcoming expedition, I should have some experience in battle so as not to slow down the group.” I replied.

It was a good excuse, and it would also work on the knightess standing next to Elara.

I didn’t glance at her, of course. I didn’t care one bit about her for now. I needed power and money. And the best way to do that was to hunt and kill monsters.

Now, unfortunately, there wasn’t a monster-infested forest around here, but there were dungeons.

Permanent dungeons.

The rewards were lower, and there might be some competitors there, but it wasn’t an issue for me. I would only be showing one or two kills at most, while keeping the rest in my inventory, so as far as anyone else would be concerned, I was just a newbie who wasn’t very good.

Elara nodded, thinking for a while before she asked, “I assume your ability doesn’t work for you.”

It was an ambiguous statement that gave away nothing. Even if it gave a hint, it could be anything. Such a buff or a debuff ability. The possibilities were endless, and it might only make me a little special, not enough for the knightess to report me back when she had reports to make on Elara.

“No, it does not, my lady.”

“Hmm… Makes sense.” She replied. She thought for a while and then finally said…

“Very well. We own a few dungeons for training. You can use one. Valeria will guide you to a few knights who can take you there.” Elara said without sparing me another glance, or giving anything away that she might treasure me as a talent or anything.

“I cannot express my gratitude enough, my lady.” Of course, I had to keep up appearances and appear as subservient and thankful as I could.

“Mhm.” Of course, the one receiving my thanks didn’t spare me a single glance.

And so, Valeria guided me outside the western wing to a barrack where a knight and a few soldiers were training. I had some fun with her on the way, but nothing too extreme, not wanting to delay my plans because God knows that ass could make even Hit*er a pacifist.

Changing my plans for the day wouldn’t be difficult.
